summary: The test suite has good organizational structure with strong end-to-end coverage via act workflow tests, but significant gaps remain. Strengths: parser tests cover both JUnit XML and JSON formats, aggregation logic (flaky detection, totals) is well-tested, workflow structure is validated, and three fixture-based act tests provide real-world E2E coverage. Critical gaps: no unit tests for src/main.ts (CLI parsing, loadRuns, --fail-on-failure flag), missing parser edge cases (XML entity decoding is coded but untested, time/duration field fallback untested, field defaults untested), markdown tests miss Failing Tests section verification (though it is tested in act), no boundary condition testing (empty inputs, zero counts, special characters), and only 3 act fixture scenarios. The act tests are the suite's strongest element but only three cases limits confidence in fixture handling across matrix builds. Would catch parsing regressions and workflow structure issues but miss CLI errors, markdown escaping edge cases, and error paths.
